summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root whose contents include GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) in a sources/targets context. When npm encounters a binding.gyp it implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` during install, and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` directives by spawning a shell to execute the embedded command during the configure step. This results in arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine on a default `npm install`, with no declared `install`/`postinstall` script required. The behavior is functionally equivalent to a lifecycle hook and is a known covert install-time RCE pattern. Additional context: the package's traced contents triggered the upstream model's malware-output safety filter, a corroborating signal of hostile code shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
